Files.HotstringLibrary.label: Hotstring Library
Files.HotstringLibrary.help:
Select the AHK file containing your custom hotstrings.This file is loaded by AutoCorrect2 on startup.  It is also read and used by several other tools.

NOTE: This string is hard-coded as an '#Include' item in AutocCorrect2.ahk.  Edit with caution.

Files.NewTemporaryHotstrLib.label: New Temporary Hotstring Library
Files.NewTemporaryHotstrLib.help: Name of file for the Updater Tool to write to.  Used for comparison and merging, when updating to a newly-released verions of the hotstring library.  Having a file name defined here will allow the Unique String Extrator to instantly run its scan without the need to manually choose the file each time.

Files.BoilerplateHotstringLibrary.label: Separate Library For Boilerplate Hotstrings
Files.BoilerplateHotstringLibrary.help:
If the HotstringHelper.SeparateLibForBoilerplates=1, then radio buttons ()AC ()BP will appear on the HH gui.  When BP is selected, then the Append button will append the new hotstring to this file.

NOTE: This string is hard-coded as an '#Include' item in AutocCorrect2.ahk.  Edit with caution.

Files.RemovedHsFile.label: Removed Hotstrings File
Files.RemovedHsFile.help: Text file that logs hotstrings you've removed or disabled.  This is accessed by Hotstring Helper's validation tool and also by MCLogger.

Files.AutoCorrectsLogFile.label: AutoCorrects Log
Files.AutoCorrectsLogFile.help: Text file where AutoCorrect2 logs every correction it makes. Used for analysis.  Gets culled via ACLogAnalyzer.  If 'EnableLogging' is turned off, then this file doesn't get used.

Files.ACLogContinuousFile.label: Auto Corrects Continuous Log
Files.ACLogContinuousFile.help: Text file where AutoCorrect2 logs every correction it makes. Used for stACLogStatistics Tool.  Never gets culled-from, just accumulates forever.  If 'EnableLogging' is turned off, then this file doesn't get used.

Files.ErrContextLog.label: Error Context Log
Files.ErrContextLog.help:
Text file that logs context information when backspaced corrections are detected. Helps determine what was being typed when misfired corrections occur.  The file is used by the Autocorrection Log Analyzer tool.  Related settings are in the ACSystem Section
 - BeepOnContextLog
 - PrecedingWordCount
 - FollowingWordCount
 - FollowingWordTimeout

Files.ACLogAnalyzer.label: ACLogAnalyzer Executable
Files.ACLogAnalyzer.help: The Autocorrection Log Analyzer.exe executable for analyzing autocorrection logs.  A related key is ACSystem.BeepOnCorrection.

Files.WordListFile.label: Word List File
Files.WordListFile.help: A text file containing a word list used for the Exam Pane's 'Misspells' and 'Fixes' lists.  It is also used by MCLogger.

Files.FrequencyListFile.label: Frequency List File
Files.FrequencyListFile.help: CSV file with frequency data for words, used in Hotstring Helper's Exam Pane, to determine if words are high-frequency words.

Files.MCLoggerPath.label: MCLogger Executable
Files.MCLoggerPath.help: The Manual-Correction Logger executable for analyzing frequent manual corrections.

Files.MCLogFile.label: Manual Corrections Log
Files.MCLogFile.help: Text file where the MCLogger tool logs the manual corrections identified by the user's typing.

Files.SettingsManager.label: Settings Manager
Files.SettingsManager.help: Unless you are editing the metadata file directly, you are probably viewing this message in the SettingsManager application.  It has been made extensible, so that other users can use the SettingsManager.ahk script with their own projects and Settings.ini files.  I don't recommend changing this ini key value.

Files.FileManagerPath.label: File Manager Path
Files.FileManagerPath.help:
Enter the full path to your preferred file manager app exe. If blank, or path is not found, Windows Explorer will be used.
Examples:
   C:\Program Files\XYplorer\XYplorer.exe
   C:\Program Files\GPSoftware\Directory Opus\dopus.exe
   C:\Program Files\zabkat\xplorer2_ult\xplorer2_64.exe

Hotkeys.HotstringHelperActivationHotkey.label: HotstringHelper Activation Hotkey
Hotkeys.HotstringHelperActivationHotkey.help: Define the hotkey combination to open HotstringHelper. Examples: #h (Win+H), ^+h (Ctrl+Shift+H), F12

Hotkeys.AutoCorrect2EditThisScriptHk.label: AutoCorrect2 Edit This Script
Hotkeys.AutoCorrect2EditThisScriptHk.help: Hotkey for opening the AutoCorrect2.ahk script in your editor.  If you are going to use this feature, I recommend setting a preferred ahk editor in Shared/DefaultEditor.

Hotkeys.SystemUpTimeHk.label: System Up Time
Hotkeys.SystemUpTimeHk.help: It's a scriptlet that shows how long your computer has been running since the last reboot.

Hotkeys.MCLoggerRunAnalysisHotkey.label: MCLogger Run Analysis Hotkey
Hotkeys.MCLoggerRunAnalysisHotkey.help: Hotkey to open and run analysis of the MCLogger manual correction log.

Hotkeys.MCLoggerSneakPeekHotkey.label: MCLogger Sneak Peek Hotkey
Hotkeys.MCLoggerSneakPeekHotkey.help: Hotkey to get a quick preview/peek at the current MCLogger cached character presses and the captured hotstrings that are due to be logged at the next save interval.

Hotkeys.ACLogAnalyzerHk.label: ACLog Analyzer Hotkey
Hotkeys.ACLogAnalyzerHk.help: The ACLogAnalyzer tool doesn't always run in the background, so this hotkey assignment is actually in the AutoCorrect2.ahk code.  This will initial an analysis of the AutoCorrections Log.

Hotkeys.AC2HotkeyRefHotkey.label: AC2HotkeyReference Hotkey
Hotkeys.AC2HotkeyRefHotkey.help: This is the hotkey for the Hotkey "Cheatsheet" application.  By default the dialog is shown at startup, but this can be disabled in the ahk code of the app.

Shared.DefaultEditor.label: Default Editor
Shared.DefaultEditor.help: Executable name of your preferred text editor.  The .ahk scripts will use VScode if it is in the default install location.  If not, then this 'backup default' will be used.

Shared.LightGreen.label: Light Green Color
Shared.LightGreen.help: Hex color code (without #) for Green elements in the UI, when a dark gui color is used.  Mostly this is used for the HH Validation report and for the progress bars on some tools.

Shared.DarkGreen.label: Dark Green Color
Shared.DarkGreen.help: Hex color code (without #) for Green elements in the UI, when a light gui color is used.  Mostly this is used for the HH Validation report and for the progress bars on some tools.

Shared.LightRed.label: Light Red Color
Shared.LightRed.help: Hex color code (without #) for Red elements in the UI, when a dark gui color is used.  Mostly this is used for the HH Validation report.

Shared.DarkRed.label: Dark Red Color
Shared.DarkRed.help: Hex color code (without #) for Red elements in the UI, when a light gui color is used.  Mostly this is used for the HH Validation report.

HotstringHelper.CODE_DEBUG_LOG.label: Debug Logging
HotstringHelper.CODE_DEBUG_LOG.help: If enabled, detailed debug information for the AutoCorrect2.ahk script is logged. Warning: very verbose output.

HotstringHelper.CODE_ERROR_LOG.label: Error Logging
HotstringHelper.CODE_ERROR_LOG.help: If enabled, errors and exceptions for the AutoCorrect2.ahk script are logged to a filein the Data folder.

HotstringHelper.EnableHotStrQuickLookup.label: Enable HotStrQuickLookup
HotstringHelper.EnableHotStrQuickLookup.help:
The Hotstring Quick Lookup Tool suplements the ACLogAnalyzer and the HotstringLibStats tools.  (If enabled) Select an entire hotstring (including the colons) and press Ctrl+Shift+Q.  Log data will be retrieved for the selected hotstring. Try it with this one:

:B0X*?:cuas::f("caus") ; Web Freq 415.77 | Fixes 61 words

HotstringHelper.EnableDragTools.label: Enable DragTools module
HotstringHelper.EnableDragTools.help: Choose whether the 'DragTools' module should be #Included.  If you already use right-click-and-drag for your file manager, or anything else, you'll want to disable this, otherwise AutoCorrect2 will hijack the hotkey.

HotstringHelper.EnableMoveResizeTools.label: Enable Window Move/Resize Tools
HotstringHelper.EnableMoveResizeTools.help: Alt+Ctrl+Left Click to drag a window from anywhere (not just titlebar). Alt+Ctrl+Right Click and drag to resize a window.  Shouldn't hijack Windows' right-click-and drag because this uses modifier keys.

HotstringHelper.DefaultFontSize.label: Default Font Size
HotstringHelper.DefaultFontSize.help: Font size (in points) for normal text in the HotstringHelper window and several other tools. Usually 10-12. Don't include the 's' at the beginning.

HotstringHelper.LargeFontSize.label: Large Font Size
HotstringHelper.LargeFontSize.help: Font size (in points) for when Trigger and Replacement boxes are toggled to 'large'.  Also used for Validity report dialog, several message boxes and several other apps.  Usually 2-4 points larger than default.  Don't include the 's' at the beginning.

HotstringHelper.DefaultWidth.label: Default Window Width
HotstringHelper.DefaultWidth.help: Width in pixels for the main HotstringHelper window when not in 'Make Bigger' mode. Recommended: 366.

HotstringHelper.HeightSizeIncrease.label: Height Increase
HotstringHelper.HeightSizeIncrease.help: Additional height (pixels) added to the window when 'Make Bigger' button is pressed to make the 'Replacement' edit box bigger.

HotstringHelper.WidthSizeIncrease.label: Width Increase
HotstringHelper.WidthSizeIncrease.help: Additional width (pixels) added to the window when 'Make Bigger' button is pressed to make the 'Replacement' edit box bigger.

HotstringHelper.InitialFocusTarget.label: HH Box Default Focus
HotstringHelper.InitialFocusTarget.help:
In the HotstringHelper dialog, which editBox should be focused by default?  Set it to one of: options, trigger, replacement, comment, auto.

If auto is selected, HH will attempt to focus the replacement box if an AutoCorrect item is detected, or the trigger box if a Boilerplate template item is detected.

HotstringHelper.DefaultBoilerPlateOpts.label: Default Boilerplate Options
HotstringHelper.DefaultBoilerPlateOpts.help: AHK trigger hotstring options applied when Hotstring Helper detect a boilerplate/template. Leave blank for none. Example: B0

HotstringHelper.BoilerplatePrefix.label: Boilerplate Prefix
HotstringHelper.BoilerplatePrefix.help: Prefix character/text added to boilerplate anagram triggers to distinguish them.

HotstringHelper.BoilerplateSuffix.label: Boilerplate Suffix
HotstringHelper.BoilerplateSuffix.help: Suffix character/text added to boilerplate anagrams. Leave blank for none.

HotstringHelper.FirstLettersToInclude.label: First Letters to Include
HotstringHelper.FirstLettersToInclude.help: When creating anagrams for boilerplate text, how many of the first letters to include from each word.  For example, the anagram for the preceding sentence is 'wcafb', if the value is 5

HotstringHelper.MinWordLength.label: Minimum Word Length
HotstringHelper.MinWordLength.help: Minimum length a word must be to include its first letters in boilerplate anagrams.  This allows us to exclude short words like 'of', 'in', 'a', etc.  If the min lengh is 2, then the anagram for the preceding sentence is 'mlwmi'.

HotstringHelper.SeparateLibForBoilerplat.label: Append Boilerplate Hotstrings to a Separate Library
HotstringHelper.SeparateLibForBoilerplat.help: If this is set to true, then radio buttons ()AC ()BP will appear on the HH gui.  When BP is selected, then the Append button will append the new hotstring to the file defined in Files.BoilerplateHotstringLibrary.  If the file is not found, one will be created.

HotstringHelper.BeepOnStartup.label: AutoCorrect2 Startup Announcement
HotstringHelper.BeepOnStartup.help:
Should there be a double-beep to indicate that the application has been restarted/reloaded?

Recommended:  Yes.

HotstringHelper.MakeFuncByDefault.label: Make Function by Default
HotstringHelper.MakeFuncByDefault.help: If enabled, new AutoCorrect entries will use the f() function wrapper by default (allows logging and context capture). If disabled, entries are added as 'plain vanilla' hotstrings, ::trigger::replacement.  The option can be turned on/off for each hotstring at runtime.  This just determines whether the '[ ] Make as f() function' checkbox is checked by default.  If this is set to true/1, then it makes sense to include 'B0X' to the DefaultAutoCorrectOptions.  Note however, that when a hotstring is wrapped in and f() function, 'B0X' will be added to the hotstring options automatically, even if the user doesn't include them in the Option box.

HotstringHelper.BeepOnFunctionCheck.label: Beep On Function Checkbox Toggle
HotstringHelper.BeepOnFunctionCheck.help: Should there be an audible beep when '[ ] Make as f() function' checkbox is checked/unchecked?

HotstringHelper.DefaultAutoCorrectOpts.label: Default AutoCorrect Options
HotstringHelper.DefaultAutoCorrectOpts.help: AHK hotstring options applied to AutoCorrect entries. If MakeFuncByDefault = 1, then these default opts should include B0X (B zero for no backspace, X for execute function). See AHK docs for all options.

HotstringHelper.AutoCommentWithFreqAndSt.label: Auto-Comment with Frequency
HotstringHelper.AutoCommentWithFreqAndSt.help: If enabled, new entries will include frequency/statistics comments. Used by the Ctrl-F3 stats dialog.  It's okay to use this feature, even if you don't use the f() functions.

HotstringHelper.AutoEnterNewEntry.label: Auto-Enter New Entry
HotstringHelper.AutoEnterNewEntry.help: If enabled, Hotstring Helper will attempt to type new AutoCorrect items into your working document after you append the item.  This only works for whole-word AutoCorrect entries.

ACSystem.EnableLogging.label: Enable Logging
ACSystem.EnableLogging.help:
Master switch - if disabled, no logging occurs for any autocorrections made by the f() function; this include the error context logger.  The Manual Correction Logger is a seprate independent tool and this setting does not affect it.

If vanilla hotstrings are used (instead of f() functions) then this setting is moot.

ACSystem.BeepOnCorrection.label: Beep on Correction
ACSystem.BeepOnCorrection.help: If enabled, a beep sound plays each time an autocorrection is made.

ACSystem.BeepOnContextLog.label: Beep on Context Log
ACSystem.BeepOnContextLog.help:
If enabled, a beep sounds when a backspaced autocorrection's context is captured and logged.  There is a bit of a delay from after backspace is pressed.  This is because the context logger is collecting the words typed after.  Related keys
 - PrecedingWordCount
 - FollowingWordCount
 - FollowingWordTimeout

ACSystem.PrecedingWordCount.label: Preceding Word Count
ACSystem.PrecedingWordCount.help: Number of words to keep cached before the autocorrection to capture in context logging. Used to understand what the user was typing when the errant autocorrection occured.  Note: digits and symbols other than period are ignored and never cached.

ACSystem.FollowingWordCount.label: Following Word Count
ACSystem.FollowingWordCount.help: Number of words after the autocorrection to wait for and capture in context logging.  Used to understand what the user was typing when the errant autocorrection occured.  Note: digits and symbols other than period are ignored and never cached.

ACSystem.FollowingWordTimeout.label: Following Word Timeout
ACSystem.FollowingWordTimeout.help: Maximum number of seconds to wait for following words to be typed before stopping context capture.

ACSystem.AppSkiplist.label: App Skiplist
ACSystem.AppSkiplist.help:
Comma-separated list of executable names where AutoCorrect should NOT run.
Do not include spaces after commas.
This:	Roblox.exe,PipeWalker.exe
Not this:	Roblox.exe, PipeWalker.exe
NOTE: This list is checked every time an AutoCorrect happens.  If there are hundreds of items, it could cause performance issues.

ACSystem.WinSkiplist.label: Window Skiplist
ACSystem.WinSkiplist.help:
Comma-separated list window titles where AutoCorrect should NOT run.
Do not include spaces after commas.
This:	First Window,Second Window
Not this:	First Window, Second Window
NOTE: This list is checked every time an AutoCorrect happens.  If there are hundreds of items, it could cause performance issues.

ACSystem.BeepOnCapFix.label: Beep on Cap Fix
ACSystem.BeepOnCapFix.help: If enabled, a beep sounds when ACcidental DOuble CAps are fixed.

ACSystem.CapFixTwoLetterWords.label: Auto Fix Double Caps on Two Letter Words
ACSystem.CapFixTwoLetterWords.help: If enabled, ACcidental DOuble CAps are fixed for common two-letter words (e.g. TO --> To).  Can be super-annoying for people who work in IT.

MCLogger.ShowEachHotString.label: Show Each Hotstring
MCLogger.ShowEachHotString.help: If enabled, a visual tooltip appears each time MCLogger captures a manual correction pattern.

MCLogger.BeepEachHotString.label: Beep Each Hotstring
MCLogger.BeepEachHotString.help: If enabled, an auditory beep sounds each time a manual correction pattern is captured.

MCLogger.SaveIntervalMinutes.label: Save Interval (Minutes)
MCLogger.SaveIntervalMinutes.help: MCLogger collects patterns in RAM and saves to disk at this interval. Smaller = more frequent saves.

MCLogger.IntervalsBeforeStopping.label: Intervals Before Stopping
MCLogger.IntervalsBeforeStopping.help: Stop collecting if no new pattern matches for this many save intervals. Collection auto-restarts on next manual correction.

MCLogger.SendToHH.label: Send to HotstringHelper
MCLogger.SendToHH.help: If enabled, The 'Append' buttons in MCLogger will export to the HotstringHelper2 GUI. If disabled, append directly to the HotstringLib.ahk file.

MCLogger.SaveFullToClipboard.label: Save Full to Clipboard
MCLogger.SaveFullToClipboard.help: If enabled, the full analysis report is also saved to Windows Clipboard. Warning: does not restore previous clipboard contents.

MCLogger.AgeOfOldSingles.label: Age of Old Singles
MCLogger.AgeOfOldSingles.help: It's not a filter for a dating website...  When removing old single-occurrence items from the Mannual Correction Log, only remove hotstrings older than this many days.

MCLogger.KeepReportOpen.label: Keep Report Open
MCLogger.KeepReportOpen.help: When clicking 'Cull and Append', close the list each time (0) or keep it open (1) for continued editing.

ACLogAnalyzer.FreqImportance.label: Frequency Importance Weight
ACLogAnalyzer.FreqImportance.help:
Importance weight (0-50) for frequency when calculating which items are most important.  The other factor is simply the ratio of backspaced, to kept, items.

0 = ignore frequency, and just sort the AC Analysis report on ratio.

ACLogAnalyzer.IgnoreFewerThan.label: Ignore Fewer Than
ACLogAnalyzer.IgnoreFewerThan.help: Minimum threshold for consideration when weighing items. Zero means consider all items regardless of frequency. For example if the value is 3, then then autocorrections which were only logged twice, won't appear on the Analysis report at all.

ACLogAnalyzer.CopyHotstringOnSelect.label: Copy Hotstring on Select
ACLogAnalyzer.CopyHotstringOnSelect.help:
If enabled, hotstring is copied to clipboard when an item is selected. Facilitates sending to HotstringHelper.
Tip: Once the item is on the clipboard, press the HH hotkey (Win-H).

ACLogAnalyzer.PinToTop.label: Pin to Top
ACLogAnalyzer.PinToTop.help: If enabled, the ACLogAnalyzer window is pinned to the top by default (stays above other windows).  Can be easily toggled via the checkbox.

ACLogAnalyzer.EnableErrorLogging.label: Enable Error Logging
ACLogAnalyzer.EnableErrorLogging.help: If enabled, ACLogAnalyzer app runtime errors are logged to file. (Not autocorrection errors, but app errors)

ACLogAnalyzer.StartLine.label: Start Line
ACLogAnalyzer.StartLine.help: When reading the log file, skip all lines before this line number. Useful if you have header/comment lines that you want to skip.

ACLogAnalyzer.CullDateFormat.label: Cull Date Format
ACLogAnalyzer.CullDateFormat.help:
Date format for the timestamp when items are culled. Examples: MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy-MM-dd, dd/MM/yyyy

See also: https://www.autohotkey.com/docs/v2/lib/FormatTime.htm#Date_Formats

Defunctionizer.keepComments.label: Keep Comments
Defunctionizer.keepComments.help:
If enabled (1), frequency/stats comments are preserved during defunctionization. If disabled (0), comments are removed. 
Notes:
 - Removing comments will break AutoCorrect2's Ctrl-F3 stats dialog
 - Comments with '...but misspells...' flags are always kept

ControlButtons.OpenLibrary.label: Open Hotstring Library
ControlButtons.OpenLibrary.help:
This is the setting for the "Open Hotstring Library" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.PersonalHotstrings.label: Open Personal Hotstrings File
ControlButtons.PersonalHotstrings.help:
This is the setting for the "Open Personal Hotstrings File" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.ACLog.label: Open AutoCorrection Log
ControlButtons.ACLog.help:
This is the setting for the "Open AutoCorrection Log" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
Note: (1) The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk and (2) if AC logging is globally disabled, then AC-related Control Buttons will always be hidden.

ControlButtons.ACAnalyze.label: Analyze AutoCorrection Log !^+Q
ControlButtons.ACAnalyze.help:
This is the setting for the "Analyze AutoCorrection Log !^+Q" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
Note: (1) The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk and (2) if AC logging is globally disabled, then AC-related Control Buttons will always be hidden.

ControlButtons.BSLog.label: Open Backspace Context Log
ControlButtons.BSLog.help:
This is the setting for the "Open Backspace Context Log" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
Note: (1) The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk and (2) if AC logging is globally disabled, then AC-related Control Buttons will always be hidden.

ControlButtons.RemovedHS.label: Open Removed HotStrings List
ControlButtons.RemovedHS.help:
This is the setting for the "Open Removed HotStrings List" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.MCLog.label: Open Manual Correction Log
ControlButtons.MCLog.help:
This is the setting for the "Open Manual Correction Log" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.MCLogContinuous.label: Open AutoCorrection Continuous Log
ControlButtons.MCLogContinuous.help:
This is the setting for the "Open AutoCorrection Continuous Log" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
Note: (1) The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk and (2) if AC logging is globally disabled, then AC-related Control Buttons will always be hidden.

ControlButtons.MCAnalyze.label: Analyze Manual Correction Log #^+Q
ControlButtons.MCAnalyze.help:
This is the setting for the "Analyze Manual Correction Log #^+Q" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.Report.label: Report HotStrings and Potential Fixes
ControlButtons.Report.help:
This is the setting for the "Report HotStrings and Potential Fixes" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.SettingsMan.label: Configuration Settings Manager
ControlButtons.SettingsMan.help:
This is the setting for the "Configuration Settings Manager" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.HotkeyRef.label: Hotkey Reference Tool
ControlButtons.HotkeyRef.help:
This is the setting for the "Hotkey Reference Tool" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.Suggester.label: Hotstring Suggester Tool
ControlButtons.Suggester.help:
This is the setting for the "Hotstring Suggester Tool" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.ExtractMisspellings.label: Extract Potential Misspellings
ControlButtons.ExtractMisspellings.help:
This is the setting for the "Extract Potential Misspellings" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.ConflictLocator.label: Conflicting String Locator Tool
ControlButtons.ConflictLocator.help:
This is the setting for the "Conflicting String Locator Tool" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.Updater.label: Check GitHub for Updates
ControlButtons.Updater.help:
This is the setting for the "Check GitHub for Updates" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.UniqueStringExtractor.label: Compare Two Versions of HotstringLib
ControlButtons.UniqueStringExtractor.help:
This is the setting for the "Compare Two Versions of HotstringLib" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.Defunctionizer.label: Defunctionize Hotstrings
ControlButtons.Defunctionizer.help:
This is the setting for the "Defunctionize Hotstrings" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)

ControlButtons.OpenFolder.label: Go to AutoCorrect2 Folder
ControlButtons.OpenFolder.help:
This is the setting for the "Go to AutoCorrect2 Folder" button in the Control Pane.  There should be two parts, separated by a comma.
- First part: 1/0 where one includes the button in the Control Pane.
- Second part: stay/close where stay means that the Hotstring Helper dialog should stay open after the button is pressed.
(Note: The specifics of what the button does is hard-coded in AutoCorrect2.ahk.)
